annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem (Śulba Sūtras)

anchor.snippet: of an oblong (rectangle) produces both

annotations.note: Mathlib states the Pythagorean theorem as a right-angle characterization in general inner-product/Euclidean spaces.

annotations.label: Baudhayana Pythagorean triples

anchor.snippet: which contains examples of simple Pythagorean triples

annotations.note: Mathlib defines the general PythagoreanTriple predicate and classifies them, covering specific triples like (3,4,5).

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem for a square (Baudhayana)

anchor.snippet: produces an area double the size of the original square

annotations.note: The equal-legs case (diagonal of a square has squared length twice the side) follows from the general Pythagorean theorem but the area-doubling statement is not separately stated.

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem for a rectangle (Baudhayana)

anchor.snippet: makes an area which the vertical and horizontal sides make together

annotations.note: This is exactly the Pythagorean theorem, formalized in general Euclidean spaces.

annotations.label: Approximation of the square root of two (Baudhayana)

anchor.snippet: Baudhayana gives an expression for the square root of two

annotations.note: Mathlib has √2 and irrational_sqrt_two but not Baudhayana's specific rational approximation 1+1/3+1/(3·4)−1/(3·4·34).

annotations.label: Construction of Meru-prastāra (Pascal's triangle)

anchor.snippet: the sum of the digits in the two squares lying above it

annotations.note: Nat.choose_succ_succ is exactly Pascal's rule: each entry equals the sum of the two entries above it.

annotations.label: Combinatorial identity (Pingala)

anchor.snippet: Pingala was aware of the combinatorial identity

annotations.note: Nat.sum_range_choose proves ∑ₖ C(n,k) = 2ⁿ, the row-sum identity.

annotations.label: Classification of numbers (Jain)

anchor.snippet: classify numbers into three classes: enumerable, innumerable and infinite

annotations.note: This historical tripartite classification is not a Mathlib notion, though it loosely echoes finite/countable/uncountable cardinality.

annotations.label: Five types of infinity (Jain)

anchor.snippet: their texts define five different types of infinity

annotations.note: The Jain fivefold classification of infinity has no counterpart in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Factorials (Anuyogadwara Sutra)

anchor.snippet: the earliest known description of factorials in Indian mathematics

annotations.note: Nat.factorial is the standard recursive definition of n!.

annotations.label: Bhuta-sankhya object numbers

anchor.section: Numerals and the decimal number system
anchor.snippet: later labelled Bhuta-sankhya

annotations.note: This word-numeral notation system is not a formal mathematical object in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bakhshali equalisation problem

anchor.snippet: Find the price of each animal and the total value for the animals possessed by each merchant

annotations.note: This specific linear word problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trigonometric functions (Surya Siddhanta)

anchor.snippet: uses the following as trigonometric functions for the first time

annotations.note: Mathlib defines Real.sin/Real.cos (and versine = 1−cos is derivable), the modern unit-radius forms of jya/kojya/utkrama-jya.

annotations.label: Value of π (Aryabhata)

anchor.snippet: The value of π , correct to 4 decimal places

annotations.note: Real.pi_gt_d4 (3.1415 < π) with Real.pi_lt_d4 (π < 3.1416) establishes π to four decimals.

annotations.label: Definition of sine (Aryabhata)

anchor.snippet: Defined the sine ( jya ) as the modern relationship between half an angle and half a chord

annotations.note: Real.sin is the modern sine function corresponding to Aryabhata's jya (up to radius scaling).

annotations.label: Sine, cosine and versine tables (Aryabhata)

anchor.snippet: Contains the earliest tables of sine, cosine and versine values

annotations.note: Mathlib has the trig functions but no numerical sine/cosine/versine value tables.

annotations.label: Sine difference formula (Aryabhata)

anchor.snippet: Contains the trigonometric formula

annotations.note: Aryabhata's second-order finite-difference recurrence for the sine table is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sum of cubes formula (Aryabhata)

anchor.snippet: The formula for the sum of the cubes

annotations.note: No closed-form lemma for ∑i³ exists in Mathlib (only the linear Gauss sum Finset.sum_range_id).

annotations.label: General solution of the indeterminate linear equation (Aryabhata)

anchor.snippet: General solution of the indeterminate linear equation

annotations.note: Bézout's identity (Int.gcd_eq_gcd_ab) provides a particular solution to ax+by=gcd, but the parametrized general solution of the kuṭṭaka is not stated.

annotations.label: Sine-cosine relations (Varahamihira)

anchor.snippet: the following formulas relating sine and cosine functions

annotations.note: The Pythagorean identity sin²+cos²=1 and complementary-angle relations are all in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's theorem

anchor.snippet: always bisects the opposite side

annotations.note: Brahmagupta's theorem on perpendicular diagonals of a cyclic quadrilateral is not formalized.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's formula

anchor.snippet: of a cyclic quadrilateral with sides of lengths

annotations.note: The area formula for a cyclic quadrilateral is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's theorem on rational triangles

anchor.snippet: A triangle with rational sides

annotations.note: Brahmagupta's construction of rational triangles with rational area is not formalized.

annotations.label: Arithmetic rules for zero and negatives (Brahmagupta)

anchor.snippet: rules for arithmetical operations involving zero and negative numbers

annotations.note: These are basic ring/field lemmas in Mathlib (mul_zero, add_zero, neg_neg, neg_mul, etc.).

annotations.label: Solution of the quadratic equation (Brahmagupta)

anchor.snippet: the first explicit (although still not completely general) solution of the quadratic equation

annotations.note: quadratic_eq_zero_iff gives the quadratic formula over a general field.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's identity

anchor.snippet: which was a generalisation of an earlier identity of Diophantus

annotations.note: This is exactly Brahmagupta's identity (a²+nb²)(c²+nd²)=… stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's lemma (Pell's equation)

anchor.snippet: Lemma (Brahmagupta)

annotations.note: Mathlib develops Pell's equation x²−dy²=1 and the composition/group structure of its solutions, but not Brahmagupta's general composition lemma for x²−Ny²=k.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's theorem on Pell's equation

anchor.snippet: has an integer solution for any one of

annotations.note: Mathlib's Pell theory covers solvability of x²−dy²=1 but not Brahmagupta's auxiliary-equation generation result.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's Pell equation example

anchor.snippet: Find integers

annotations.note: This specific numerical Pell instance is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sine of an acute angle without a table (Bhaskara I)

anchor.snippet: A formula for calculating the sine of an acute angle without the use of a table

annotations.note: Bhaskara I's rational sine approximation formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ardhaccheda (Virasena)

anchor.snippet: the concept of ardhaccheda , the number of times a number could be halved

annotations.note: Nat.log 2 captures the binary logarithm / number of halvings for powers of two; the 2-adic valuation is also available.

annotations.label: Volume of a frustum (Virasena)

anchor.snippet: The derivation of the volume of a frustum by a sort of infinite procedure

annotations.note: No frustum volume formula is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Area of an ellipse (Mahavira)

anchor.snippet: Formulae derived to calculate the area of an ellipse and quadrilateral inside a circle

annotations.note: Mathlib has no explicit area-of-ellipse (πab) lemma.

annotations.label: Square root of a negative number (Mahavira)

anchor.snippet: Asserted that the square root of a negative number did not exist

annotations.note: Mathlib encodes this via sq_nonneg (no real has a negative square) and Real.sqrt returning 0 on negatives, but there is no single lemma phrased as 'no real square root of a negative'.

annotations.label: Sum of squares of an arithmetic progression (Mahavira)

anchor.snippet: the sum of a series whose terms are squares of an arithmetical progression

annotations.note: No closed-form lemma for the sum of squares of an arithmetic progression exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: General solutions of higher order polynomials (Mahavira)

anchor.snippet: Gave the general solutions of the higher order polynomial equations

annotations.note: No such general polynomial solution is formalized (and Mathlib's Abel–Ruffini shows it is impossible in general).

annotations.label: Volume of a sphere (Shridhara)

anchor.snippet: A good rule for finding the volume of a sphere

annotations.note: EuclideanSpace.volume_ball gives the n-dimensional ball volume (yielding 4/3·π·r³ for n=3) but the 3-D sphere formula is not a separately stated lemma.

annotations.label: Solving quadratic equations (Shridhara)

anchor.snippet: The formula for solving quadratic equations

annotations.note: quadratic_eq_zero_iff is the general quadratic formula.

annotations.label: Derivative of sine (Manjula)

anchor.snippet: who realised that the expression

annotations.note: Real.deriv_sin proves deriv sin = cos.

annotations.label: Early form of Rolle's theorem (Bhaskara II)

anchor.snippet: Stated early form of Rolle's theorem

annotations.note: exists_deriv_eq_zero is the full statement of Rolle's theorem.

annotations.label: Cosine as derivative of sine (Bhaskara II)

anchor.snippet: This can be interpreted as the discovery that cosine is the derivative of sine

annotations.note: Real.deriv_sin = cos is exactly the statement that cosine is the derivative of sine.

annotations.label: Value of π to five places (Bhaskara II)

anchor.snippet: Computed π , correct to five decimal places

annotations.note: Real.pi_gt_d6 (3.141592 < π) and Real.pi_lt_d6 (π < 3.141593) pin π to well beyond five decimals.

annotations.label: Proof of the Pythagorean theorem (Bhaskara II)

anchor.snippet: Gave a proof of the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem is proved in Mathlib for general Euclidean spaces.

annotations.label: A positive number has two square roots (Bhaskara II)

anchor.snippet: The recognition of a positive number having two square roots

annotations.note: mul_self_eq_mul_self_iff (a·a=b·b ↔ a=b ∨ a=−b) expresses that a square has exactly the two roots ±√.

annotations.label: Division by zero is infinity (Bhaskara II)

anchor.snippet: Gave a proof for division by zero being infinity

annotations.note: Mathlib adopts the opposite convention, div_zero: x/0 = 0, so this claim is not formalized.

annotations.label: Trigonometric formulas (Bhaskara II)

anchor.snippet: The trigonometric formulas

annotations.note: The sine/cosine addition formulas sin(a±b), cos(a±b) are in Mathlib (Real.sin_add, Real.cos_add).

annotations.label: Series expansions for trig functions (Kerala)

anchor.snippet: The most important results, series expansion for trigonometric functions

annotations.note: Real.hasSum_sin/Real.hasSum_cos give the power-series expansions of sine and cosine.

annotations.label: Infinite geometric series (Kerala)

anchor.snippet: The (infinite) geometric series

annotations.note: tsum_geometric_of_lt_one sums the geometric series ∑rⁿ = (1−r)⁻¹.

annotations.label: Sum of powers for large n (Kerala)

anchor.snippet: A semi-rigorous proof

annotations.note: Faulhaber's formula (Finset.sum_range_pow) gives exact power sums via Bernoulli numbers, but not the Kerala asymptotic ∑iᵏ ≈ nᵏ⁺¹/(k+1).

annotations.label: Taylor–Maclaurin series for sin, cos, arctan (Kerala)

anchor.snippet: infinite series for sin x, cos x, and arctan x

annotations.note: Mathlib has the series for sin and cos (Real.hasSum_sin/cos) and for arctan (Real.hasSum_arctan).

annotations.label: Leibniz formula for π (Kerala)

anchor.snippet: to obtain the Leibniz formula for π

annotations.note: Real.tendsto_sum_pi_div_four is exactly the Madhava–Leibniz series 1−1/3+1/5−… = π/4.

annotations.label: Rational approximation of π (Kerala)

anchor.snippet: Using the improved series to derive a rational expression

annotations.note: The Kerala-school improved (error-corrected) rational expression for π is not formalized.

annotations.label: Differentiation of trig functions (Kerala)

anchor.snippet: method of differentiation of some trigonometric functions

annotations.note: Derivatives of the trig functions (Real.deriv_sin, Real.deriv_cos) are formalized.

annotations.label: Integer solutions to 21 systems (Citrabhanu)

anchor.snippet: gave integer solutions to 21 types of systems of two simultaneous algebraic equations in two unknowns

annotations.note: Citrabhanu's catalogue of 21 specific two-variable systems is not formalized.

annotations.label: Version of the mean value theorem (Parameshvara)

anchor.snippet: a version of the mean value theorem

annotations.note: exists_hasDerivAt_eq_slope is Lagrange's mean value theorem.

annotations.label: Cows and calves problem (Narayana)

anchor.snippet: How many cows and calves are there altogether after 20 years

annotations.note: This specific word problem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Narayana's recurrence sequence

anchor.snippet: Translated into the modern mathematical language of recurrence sequences

annotations.note: Narayana's cows sequence (a_n = a_{n-1}+a_{n-3}) is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Supergolden ratio limit (Narayana)

anchor.snippet: The limit ratio between consecutive terms is the supergolden ratio

annotations.note: Mathlib has goldenRatio but neither the supergolden ratio nor this limit result.
